text:
There once was a young boy named Toby, who wanted nothing more than to fly as easily as the birds. Every day he would sneak into his grandfather’s attic, where ancient feathers and antique wings lay silently. One night, while exploring, Toby found a peculiar-looking bird feather, woven with tiny silver beads that glimmered softly. He dreamed every night of wings that fluttered under his feet.

After many years of longing, Toby decided to make his own wings. He scoured fields and forests, collecting feathers from rare birds—each feather telling a story of flight. One rainy evening, he crafted a wing kit from bird bone and feathers he found. When he was finally able to put them on, he stood on the edge of a lake at twilight.

With quiet reverence, Toby spread his wings and took flight for the first time. He felt weightless – a bird in paradise. He flew south, searching for a place wise enough to appreciate his flights. He visited meadows where the
